titleStmt.title: Letter from S. A. Arthur to Sir Robert Chalmers, the Under-Secretary for Ireland, 13 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: S. A. Arthur

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from S. A. Arthur to the under secretary of Ireland referring to financial management of Miss Quin and Miss Flanagan who are residing in a lunatic asylum in Caen. Enclosed is a letter from the Foreign Office to the same effect.At the time this letter was written Sir Robert Chalmers (1858 - 1938) was serving as the Under-Secretary for Ireland. Chalmers was sent to Ireland as a replacement for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5618/12070/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HOME OFFICE, WHITEHALL. 13th July, 1916. Sir, I am directed by the Secretary of State to send,
herewith, to be dealt with by the Irish Government, a letter
and enclosures which he has received from the Foreign Office
regarding the money owing for the keep of two ladies, Miss
Flanagan and Miss Quin, in a Lunatic Asylum at Caen. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, S.A. Arthur The Under Secretary, DUBLIN CASTLE I.G. For report as to
the relative of the two ladies. PCuF
24âVIIâ16 Under Secretary,
Reports regarding the two cases
are attached.
Reference to previous correspondence: â Letter to from Foreign Office: The UnderâSecretary of State for Foreign Affairs presents
his compliments to the Under Secretary of State for the
Home Department and is directed by the Secretary of
State for Foreign Affairs to transmit the accompanying copy of
two despatches which have been received from Hill
Consul General at Havre concerning
owing for the keep of Mrs Quin
and Mrs Flanagan now at L'Aside d'Alienes
du Bondaureut de Caen. The Secretary of State would be glad to be advised
what answer should be returned to Mr Churchill. Foreign Office 26th June, 1916. (Similar letter sent to
